# Anabella Migration — Critique of v1 Plan & Improved Automated Process

**Context:** `MIGRATION_PLAN_ANABELLA.md` (generated by Gemini) was the roadmap for rebuilding `https://www.anabelladentistry.com/` as `https://anabelladentistry-web.vercel.app/`. The shipped Vercel build does not mirror the live site. This document explains *why*, fixes the faulty assumptions, and replaces the plan with an automated, verifiable process.

---

## 1. Why v1 Fell Short — Concrete Gap Analysis

### 1.1 Scope: the original plan ignored ~60 pages
The Gemini plan scopes "the homepage" and implies component reconstruction, but the live site has ~60 URLs spanning six content trees (General, Cosmetic, Family, Restorative, Preventative, Patient Info) plus policy pages. The current Astro repo ships **1 page** (`src/pages/index.astro`) and **4 components**. Every link in the header (`/about/meet-the-team/`, `/general/checkups/`, etc.) points to routes that don't exist and also don't match the real WordPress permalinks.

### 1.2 URL mismatch — the nav uses invented paths
The real site uses flat permalinks like `/us/`, `/dental-exams/`, `/dental-cavities-solutions/`, `/veneers/`, `/tmj-treatment/`. The Astro `Header.astro` uses nested, invented ones like `/about/meet-the-team/`, `/general/checkups/`, `/cosmetic/veneers/`, `/tmj/`. Even once pages exist, links will 404.

### 1.3 Design tokens are wrong
`src/styles/global.css` is labeled "Anabella Exact Technical Palette" but the values are imaginary:

| Token | Repo value | Real site value |
|---|---|---|
| Gold accent | `#C5A059` | **No gold.** Real accent is sage green `#c1dbc7`, hover teal `#72b7bc` |
| Header background | Linear gradient `#aebcd1 → #536d92 → #aebcd1` (sage *blue*) | Solid `#333333` dark charcoal |
| Top bar background | `#808080` | `#333333` |
| Body text | `#333333` | `#424242` |
| Heading font | Playfair Display (per `Layout.astro`) / Raleway (per `global.css`) — contradict each other | **Raleway** (600/300/400/700) |
| Body font | Inter (per `Layout.astro`) / Lato (per `global.css`) — contradict each other | **Lato** (400) |
| Undefined var | `--color-bg-warm` used by `index.astro` and components, never declared | n/a — eliminate or define |

The font mismatch alone means the shipped site looks nothing like the original no matter how the rest of the CSS is tuned.

### 1.4 Hero, copy, and trust signals are fabricated
* Hero H1 in repo: "Where Healthy Smiles Begin" (a big h1 in a glassmorphism card over a stock photo). Real hero: H1 is "Dentist Bellevue WA", tagline is "Where Healthy Smiles Begin" (h3), with Dr. Clara H. Song's portrait alongside — no glass card, no stock photo.
* Hero background in repo: hotlinked `dental.inceptionimages.com/...smiling-woman-2000x1100.jpg` (not used by the real site at all).
* `TrustSignals.astro` fabricates claims: "Voted Best of Seattle 10+ Consecutive Years", "Diamond Provider CandidPro Clear Aligners", "30+ Years Experience". Only the "30+ years" is supported by the real site ("For over three decades"). The other two are invented marketing claims that could cause reputational / legal issues for a real practice.
* Service grid card labeled `TOOTH PAIN` — real site says **Dental Cavities**.
* Sleep Apnea and TMJ blurbs are paraphrased from the real copy instead of preserved verbatim.

### 1.5 Assets are hotlinked from the legacy host
Every image in the repo points back to `anabelladentistry.com/wp-content/...` or `dental.inceptionimages.com/...`. Consequences:
* If the legacy host is taken down (the whole point of this migration), the new site breaks.
* Defeats the "Edge" performance pitch — assets still traverse WordPress, no WebP/AVIF conversion, no Astro `<Image />` optimization.
* Violates the plan's own Phase 3.3 ("Use Astro's `<Image />` to serve WebP/AVIF").

### 1.6 Site-essential features are absent
* Contact section with the real address (**11216 NE 15th St Suite A, Bellevue, WA 98004**), phone `(425) 454-3221`, hours (Mon–Wed 9–5, Thu 7–3:30, Fri/Sat/Sun closed).
* Google Maps embed centered on 47.6245, -122.1901.
* Testimonials (three real quotes: Nut L., Ali J., B Picha S.).
* Footer with policy links (accessibility, HIPAA, privacy, anti-discrimination, healthcare disclaimer, terms, good-faith estimate, AI disclaimer) — all legally important for a medical practice.
* ZocDoc booking link / Schedule CTA.
* Yoast-style SEO metadata + JSON-LD LocalBusiness schema (currently just a `<title>`).
* `sitemap.xml`, `robots.txt`, favicon webp set.

### 1.7 CLAUDE.md says "Kirkland, WA"
The repo's `CLAUDE.md` describes the practice as located in **Kirkland, WA**. The real address is **Bellevue, WA**. Somewhere upstream the project picked up bad context, and it's shaping everything downstream (content, SEO, nav).

### 1.8 The v1 plan itself is a slide, not a plan
Looking at `MIGRATION_PLAN_ANABELLA.md` line-by-line:

| Plan item | What's missing |
|---|---|
| 1.1 "Design Audit" | No deliverable, no artifact. Result: palette & fonts were guessed, not audited. |
| 1.2 "Design System Extraction" | No tool named, no source captured. No `tokens.json`. |
| 1.3 "Asset Scoping" | No manifest of images. Assets were never downloaded. |
| 2.2 "Global Theming" | Theme was written by hand with invented values. |
| 3.1 "Structural Scaffolding" | For which pages? Only homepage was built. |
| 3.2 "CSS Modernization" | No source CSS was extracted to modernize. |
| 3.3 "Image Overhaul" | Requires assets; none exist locally. |
| 4.1 "Hero Optimization" | Replaced a non-existent slider with a wrong hero. |
| 4.3 "No-Code Integration (Sanity)" | Scope creep — not needed for a visual-fidelity PoC. |
| 5.1 "Lighthouse 95+" | Measurable, fine — but only for the one page that exists. |
| Success metric ">95% UI similarity" | No measurement methodology. |

The plan had no acceptance criteria tied to *the actual content of the site*. So the result could be rubber-stamped without noticing it was the wrong site.

---

## 2. Principles for v2

1. **Source of truth is the live site, captured once.** Scrape all HTML + assets to disk, work against that snapshot, not memory.
2. **Data and presentation are separated.** Content (copy, images, links) lives in JSON; Astro components render it. A rebuild can be re-run anytime from the JSON.
3. **Every page gets a row.** A spreadsheet (`migration-manifest.csv`) tracks: old URL → new route → status → checksum. No page is "done" until its row is green.
4. **Automated, repeatable, verifiable.** Four scripts (`crawl`, `extract`, `download-assets`, `generate`) run end-to-end. Re-running should produce a byte-identical result.
5. **Visual diff is an acceptance gate.** For every page, a Playwright screenshot at 3 viewports gets pixel-diffed against the live site's same viewport. "Looks about right" is not acceptance.
6. **No invented copy, no invented claims.** Only what the real site says, verbatim. Marketing can be improved in a follow-up pass, not during fidelity migration.

---

## 3. Improved Automated Process (v2)

### Stage 0 — Reset assumptions (half-day)
* Fix `CLAUDE.md` (Bellevue, not Kirkland).
* Fix `global.css` design tokens and `Layout.astro` font imports so they agree.
* Delete the placeholder hero/services content and the invented trust signals. Replace with a stub that renders from `src/data/site.json`.

### Stage 1 — Capture (automated, one script)
`tools/crawl.mjs`
* Starts at `https://www.anabelladentistry.com/`.
* Parses nav + submenus + footer + inline links to discover URLs.
* Stays on-host, follows up to depth 3, filters out `/wp-admin/`, `/feed/`, `?s=`, `wp-json`, etc.
* Saves every HTML response to `./tools/snapshot/<slug>.html`.
* Emits `./tools/snapshot/urls.json` — the canonical list of pages.
* Emits `./tools/snapshot/assets.json` — every `wp-content/uploads/...` URL referenced anywhere.

### Stage 2 — Asset mirror (automated)
`tools/download-assets.mjs`
* Reads `assets.json`, downloads each file into `public/assets/<path>`.
* Re-runs `cwebp`/`sharp` to produce WebP + AVIF variants where useful.
* Emits `./tools/snapshot/asset-map.json` mapping `https://www.anabelladentistry.com/wp-content/...` → `/assets/...`.

### Stage 3 — Content extraction (automated)
`tools/extract.mjs`
* Reads each snapshotted HTML file.
* Uses `cheerio` to parse. Strips BeaverBuilder/jQuery/Yoast chrome. Keeps:
  * Title, meta description, canonical, OG image.
  * Breadcrumbs.
  * All headings + body prose in order.
  * Images (rewritten via `asset-map.json`).
  * CTAs (buttons + hrefs, rewritten to local routes).
  * JSON-LD blocks (preserved so SEO schema comes along).
* Writes `src/data/pages/<slug>.json` — one JSON per page.
* Writes `src/data/site.json` — site-wide config (nav, footer, hours, address, phone, reviews, policy links).
* Writes `tools/snapshot/migration-manifest.csv` with per-page status.

### Stage 4 — Page generation (automated)
`tools/generate.mjs`
* For every JSON in `src/data/pages/`, writes `src/pages/<route>.astro`. Routes are derived from the real permalink (so `/dental-cavities-solutions/` → `src/pages/dental-cavities-solutions/index.astro`). No invented paths.
* Each page imports `Layout.astro` + a small set of section components (`<Prose>`, `<Hero>`, `<ServiceGrid>`, `<Contact>`, `<Testimonials>`) and passes the JSON in as props.
* Uses Astro's `<Image />` for every `img` so WebP/AVIF + responsive srcset are produced at build time.

### Stage 5 — Enhancement (manual, small)
* Replace the placeholder Google Maps iframe with a deferred-load embed.
* Wire the "Schedule" button to the real ZocDoc URL (copied from live site).
* Add `sitemap.xml` via `@astrojs/sitemap`.
* Add `robots.txt`.
* Inject the preserved JSON-LD into each page head.

### Stage 6 — Verification (automated, gate)
`tools/verify.mjs`
* Spins up `npm run preview` on `localhost:4321`.
* For every URL in `urls.json`, loads both the local page and the live URL in Playwright at widths 375/768/1440.
* Captures screenshots, computes `pixelmatch` diff, writes `tools/snapshot/diff-report.html` with side-by-side thumbnails.
* Fails if any page has > 3% diff or any broken internal link.

### Stage 7 — Deploy
* Push to `anabelladentistry-web` on GitHub.
* Vercel build runs `astro build` → static export → Edge network.
* Run Lighthouse on a sample of 5 pages, archive JSON reports to `tools/lighthouse-<date>/`.

---

## 4. Acceptance Criteria (replacing v1's "95% UI similarity")

A page is **accepted** only when all of:
1. `migration-manifest.csv` row shows `status=done`.
2. Visual diff against the live page at 1440px < 3% (pixelmatch).
3. All internal `<a href>` resolve to a generated route (no 404).
4. All images resolve under `/assets/...` (no external hotlinks).
5. Lighthouse mobile Performance ≥ 90, Accessibility ≥ 95, SEO ≥ 95.
6. JSON-LD from the live page is present in the new page head.

A build is **shippable** only when ≥ 95% of pages in the manifest are accepted, policy/legal pages are 100% accepted, and the homepage + contact + schedule flow are 100% accepted.

---

## 5. What changes in the repo right now

* New folder `tools/` with the four scripts above (`crawl`, `download-assets`, `extract`, `generate`, `verify`).
* New folder `src/data/` with `site.json` and `pages/` holding one JSON per route.
* `src/pages/index.astro` refactored to render from `site.json` + `pages/home.json`.
* `Layout.astro` and `global.css` reconciled on Raleway + Lato + the real color palette.
* `Header.astro` reads nav from `site.json` so every link is a real route.
* `TrustSignals.astro` rewritten to show only real, verifiable claims (30+ years, real insurance list, real testimonial count). Nothing invented.
* `public/assets/` populated by `download-assets.mjs` (not committed to git until first run).
* `MIGRATION_PLAN_ANABELLA.md` superseded by this file; kept in history for context.

---

## 6. Timeline (realistic, PoC-scoped)

| Day | Deliverable |
|---|---|
| 0 | Tokens/fonts/URLs fixed. Placeholder content removed. Toolchain scaffolded. |
| 1 | Crawl + download-assets runs clean. `snapshot/` populated for all ~60 URLs. |
| 2 | Extract produces one JSON per page. Manifest generated. |
| 3 | Generate produces one `.astro` per route. Site builds cleanly. |
| 4 | Verify passes ≥ 90% pages. Fix regressions, re-run. |
| 5 | Lighthouse pass. Deploy. Share preview link. |

Five working days, not bullet points. The difference from v1 is that each day has a runnable artifact and a measurable gate.
